title: APIv2: Filters in /containers/json do not work
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**



**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. I have two running containers and one exited:
```
 $ sudo podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND  CREATED        STATUS                    PORTS                                           NAMES
e9587d61ca01  docker.io/library/fedora:latest  bash     3 seconds ago  Exited (0) 3 seconds ago                                                  pedantic_grothendieck
a3de7a776439  docker.io/library/mongo:latest   mongod   28 hours ago   Up 28 hours ago           0.0.0.0:80->5000/tcp, 0.0.0.0:27017->27017/tcp  mongo
5301fa491794  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1                      28 hours ago   Up 28 hours ago           0.0.0.0:80->5000/tcp, 0.0.0.0:27017->27017/tcp  e65f6b47d346-infra
```
2. Querying containers with `/constainers/json` endpoint works as expected:
```
sudo curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.24/libpod/containers/json | jq | grep Id
    "Id": "5301fa4917945dcbf738cc9af0cfb540950b54ba31b30fd67ed7a3b839252980",
    "Id": "a3de7a77643968dea0ca503a046d73df16947711b8f4f475d260fb04b279af5d",
```

3. Querying all containers with `/constainers/json` endpoint works as expected:
```
$ sudo curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.24/libpod/containers/json?all=true | jq | grep Id
    "Id": "5301fa4917945dcbf738cc9af0cfb540950b54ba31b30fd67ed7a3b839252980",
    "Id": "a3de7a77643968dea0ca503a046d73df16947711b8f4f475d260fb04b279af5d",
    "Id": "e9587d61ca0133db4eb3d53ee2a53ef9088e597774c9f17a33f840a9ebe21822",
```

4. When I want to just see one container I user `filters="id=<container_id>"`:
```
sudo curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock "http://d/v1.24/libpod/containers/json?filters=id%3Da3de7a77643968dea0ca503a046d73df16947711b8f4f475d260fb04b279af5d" | jq | grep Id
    "Id": "5301fa4917945dcbf738cc9af0cfb540950b54ba31b30fd67ed7a3b839252980",
    "Id": "a3de7a77643968dea0ca503a046d73df16947711b8f4f475d260fb04b279af5d",
```
This is **incorrect**. It should return only one container and not both. (Although I think this was working before?)

5. When I want to filter container that is not running, I need to pass `all=true`, which reports all containers, ignoring filters as well.

Notes:

- Worth mentioning that I see this in `sudo podman --log-level=debug system service -t 0` for every query I send:
```
DEBU[0325] APIHandler -- Method: GET URL: /v1.24/libpod/containers/json?filters=id%3Da3de7a77643968dea0ca503a046d73df16947711b8f4f475d260fb04b279af5d 
INFO[0325] convertUrlValuesString: Failed to Unmarshal id=a3de7a77643968dea0ca503a046d73df16947711b8f4f475d260fb04b279af5d: invalid character 'i' looking for beginning of value
```

- I think this is also issue with `/images/json` but not tested now.

comments.body:
Ok, so it was issue with the fact that I was sanding wrongly formated filters.
From documentation it was absolutely not clear how it should look like, but I've been told by @baude that the documentation mentions it, it was just not yet deployed.
